programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The episode opens with contestant Geoff Rempel sharing his powerful backstory and the unique challenges of competing without a military or firearms background—proving that heart, not just experience, earns a place on this team. As the mission begins, the team links up with Pete from Red Cat Drones to run light recon and test critical communication between ground and aerial support. Red Cat brings the best of the best to the skies while the team takes to the ground on bikes, learning to operate under pressure with real-time air-to-ground coordination. But it’s not all strategy and tech—emotions run high as contestant Hayley Taylor opens up about her late father and the personal mission driving her to push harder every day. From aerial coverage to cover stories, it’s not just about who’s strongest... it’s about who’s ready when it counts.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The day kicks off with a one-of-a-kind “hatting ceremony” as Don Mann is crowned—then quite literally set on fire. But there’s no time to admire the flames; Ken Shamrock is waiting with another round of punishing Lion’s Den training. With Iron Man training just one day away, the pressure is at an all-time high. Contestant Landon reflects on his past Surviving Mann experience, paying tribute to the mentors who guided him—and now stepping into that role himself. The countdown to mission time begins as gear is packed, coordinates plotted, and details roll in via Red Cat Drones. With the ISR team deployed, the first phase of the mission is underway. Every contestant must be ready to deliver their cover for status in an instant—because in this game, hesitation can be the difference between success and failure.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Back by unpopular demand, the infamous “Blah Line” returns—and it’s more brutal than ever. The contestants kick off the day with a hellish PT session that leaves bodies broken and spirits tested. Things only get rougher from there with a punishing stealth crawl over jagged rocks, grinding them down before they face Ken Shamrock’s infamous Lion’s Den training—a relentless gauntlet of pain, grit, and raw endurance. As Don Mann continues to forge a battle-ready team, the squad is thrown into breaching drills designed for high-stakes mission execution. It’s chaos, it’s calculated, and it separates the contenders from the pretenders. In the heat of the action, two standouts emerge from the ranks—earning the coveted role of designated breachers.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: Contestants Stephen and Zacharias sit down to go through their experience with shooting the latest nonlethal weaponry and how their mission prep is going so far. From there, host Chris Heavens invites U.S. Arms and Athlon Optics to take a seat and show a sneak peak at their latest products. Before the end of the episode, the contestants let it slip that there’s going to be quite the surprise to come.

programme.title: Stomping Grounds
programme.desc: Host Ryan McVinney takes Boat Trader fans on an indepth exploration of Florida flats fishing deep in the backcountry of Islamorada. Featuring Captain Eric Lund, a Wall Street Broker turned backcountry fishing guide, this episode reveals the key design features of the world’s best flats skiffs and how they are built for targeting some of the most exciting sportfish species on the planet. Also, covered are bay boats and their versatility for families exploring Florida’s coastal waterways, along with a detailed study of fish species and wildlife in the region. Some of the area’s most beautiful and secret locales show why Islamorada has earned the nickname of the Sportfish Capital of The World.

programme.title: Ship Shape TV

programme.title: Stoked on Fishing - An Unforgettable Trip, South Lake, Part Two
programme.desc: This show was filmed in late October of 2019. Four months prior, in June of 2019, Tim Carnahan was diagnosed with Glioblastoma, Brain Cancer. According to Tim's medical team, his life expectancy could be between three months and 18 months. Fast forward to now, 2020; today's update is one we hoped would never come. With a heavy heart and tear-filled eyes, we share with you that Tim Carnahan has passed away after battling for close to 12 months. It only fits that the last words to pass his lips were I love you, Lory, and keep fishing. While his time on earth was cut short, the amount of joy he experienced and shared with others was genuinely tremendous and will never be forgotten. If you would like to help out in any way, lory and friends have put together a Go Fund Me page to ease the financial burden of medical bills and afford lory some time to greave and transition into life without Tim. We love you, Tim. Rest in peace, buddy.

programme.desc: One of the 7 Natural Wonders of the World, Victoria Falls is an amazing spectacle that straddles the Zambia/Zimbabwe border. programme.title: Water Ways TV Ep212: Orillia, Ontario – Canada: The Trent-Severn Waterway’s Best-Kept Secret
programme.desc: Host Steven Bull cruises into Orillia, Ontario, via the Trent-Severn Waterway, in time for their legendary pirate party and musket battle.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The episode opens with contestant Geoff Rempel sharing his powerful backstory and the unique challenges of competing without a military or firearms background—proving that heart, not just experience, earns a place on this team. As the mission begins, the team links up with Pete from Red Cat Drones to run light recon and test critical communication between ground and aerial support. Red Cat brings the best of the best to the skies while the team takes to the ground on bikes, learning to operate under pressure with real-time air-to-ground coordination. But it’s not all strategy and tech—emotions run high as contestant Hayley Taylor opens up about her late father and the personal mission driving her to push harder every day. From aerial coverage to cover stories, it’s not just about who’s strongest... it’s about who’s ready when it counts.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: The day kicks off with a one-of-a-kind “hatting ceremony” as Don Mann is crowned—then quite literally set on fire. But there’s no time to admire the flames; Ken Shamrock is waiting with another round of punishing Lion’s Den training. With Iron Man training just one day away, the pressure is at an all-time high. Contestant Landon reflects on his past Surviving Mann experience, paying tribute to the mentors who guided him—and now stepping into that role himself. The countdown to mission time begins as gear is packed, coordinates plotted, and details roll in via Red Cat Drones. With the ISR team deployed, the first phase of the mission is underway. Every contestant must be ready to deliver their cover for status in an instant—because in this game, hesitation can be the difference between success and failure.

programme.title: Surviving Mann
programme.desc: Back by unpopular demand, the infamous “Blah Line” returns—and it’s more brutal than ever. The contestants kick off the day with a hellish PT session that leaves bodies broken and spirits tested. Things only get rougher from there with a punishing stealth crawl over jagged rocks, grinding them down before they face Ken Shamrock’s infamous Lion’s Den training—a relentless gauntlet of pain, grit, and raw endurance. As Don Mann continues to forge a battle-ready team, the squad is thrown into breaching drills designed for high-stakes mission execution. It’s chaos, it’s calculated, and it separates the contenders from the pretenders. In the heat of the action, two standouts emerge from the ranks—earning the coveted role of designated breachers.

programme.title: Aftermath
programme.desc: Contestants Stephen and Zacharias sit down to go through their experience with shooting the latest nonlethal weaponry and how their mission prep is going so far. From there, host Chris Heavens invites U.S. Arms and Athlon Optics to take a seat and show a sneak peak at their latest products. Before the end of the episode, the contestants let it slip that there’s going to be quite the surprise to come.

programme.title: S1E4 The Jackson Goldstone Story
programme.desc: Beyond Limits: The Jackson Goldstone Story is an electrifying YouTube series that follows mountain biking sensation Jackson Goldstone through his 2023 UCI Downhill World Championship season. Watch as he pushes his limits on some of the most dangerous downhill courses in the world, from steep, rocky descents to twisting forest trails, attacking every challenge with unmatched skill and determination. Go behind the scenes to see the intense training sessions, precise bike tuning, and mental preparation that fuel his success. Experience the rush of high-stakes racing with heart-pounding runs, fierce rivalries, and moments that will keep you holding your breath. More than just the action, Beyond Limits reveals Jackson’s personal journey, the sacrifices he makes for his passion, and the powerful support network that drives him forward. This is the ultimate inside look at a world-class athlete chasing glory on two wheels.

programme.title: S1E5 The Jackson Goldstone Story
programme.desc: Beyond Limits: The Jackson Goldstone Story is an electrifying YouTube series that follows mountain biking sensation Jackson Goldstone through his 2023 UCI Downhill World Championship season. programme.title: S1E1 Two Roads
programme.desc:
“Two Roads” is an original GoPro series that follows nine athletes and one coach who have committed themselves to the relentless pursuit of greatness. Each episode dives deep into an athlete’s unique journey, revealing the life-changing moments that shaped the path they walk today.
This episode features Olympic bronze medalist Kristian Ipsen, American rugby union star Carlin Isles — celebrated as “The Fastest Man in Rugby” — and his remarkable rise from track and field and American football to becoming a two-time Olympian in rugby sevens. You’ll also meet Allison Stokke, a record-breaking high school pole vaulter who went on to compete at UC Berkeley, and Miles Chamley-Watson, a British-American fencing champion whose career includes 13 team Pan American titles, a 2019 team world championship, a 2013 individual world championship, three Olympic appearances, and a 2016 team Olympic bronze medal.

programme.title: S1E2 Two Roads
programme.desc: “Two Roads” is an original GoPro series that follows nine athletes and one coach who have committed themselves to the relentless pursuit of greatness. Each episode dives deep into an athlete’s unique journey, revealing the life-changing moments that shaped the path they walk today. This episode features Trenten Merrill, an American Paralympic long jumper who claimed bronze at the 2020 Summer Paralympics. Also featured is Casey Patterson, a powerhouse in professional beach volleyball with numerous AVP tournament wins, a gold medal on the FIVB World Tour, and an appearance at the 2016 Rio Olympics alongside Jake Gibb. Tennis legends Bob and Mike Bryan, the most successful doubles team in history, take the spotlight with 16 Grand Slam titles, 119 ATP Tour doubles victories, 438 weeks as the world’s No. 1 team, and Olympic gold in 2012. Rounding out the episode is acclaimed gymnastics coach Liang Chow, celebrated for guiding Gabby Douglas to the 2012 Olympic all-around gold and coaching Shawn Johnson to multiple medals at the 2008 Olympics.

programme.title: S1E3 Two Roads
programme.desc: “Two Roads” is an original GoPro series that follows nine athletes and one coach who have committed themselves to the relentless pursuit of greatness. Each episode dives deep into an athlete’s unique journey, revealing the life-changing moments that shaped the path they walk today. This episode features Paul Rabil, legendary professional lacrosse player and co-founder of the Premier Lacrosse League, celebrated as one of the greatest in the sport’s history. Also featured is Britney Henry, a standout track and field athlete from the University of Oregon who captured the Pac-10 Conference title in the hammer throw. Rounding out the episode is Meagan Martin, trailblazing competitor as the first female rookie to complete the American Ninja Warrior course and the first-ever Women’s Champion of the series.

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy: A Future Stolen: Finding Justice for Sade Robinson

programme.title: Crimes, Cons and Capers: Fundraising Scams

programme.title: Hometown Tragedy: A Mother's Nightmare: The Desperate Search for Cupcake
programme.desc: Three-year-old Kamille McKinney, affectionately known as "Cupcake," is abducted while playing outside a relative's Birmingham home. The ensuing search unifies the city, as residents pray she will be found alive.

programme.title: Ed and Day in The 'Burgh: Rowing Hard in Millvale
